title: lupinus rivularis var. viridifolius
intro: Lupinus rivularis var. viridifolius, also known as the green-leaved lupine, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the lupine plant, a type of flowering legume native to the western coast of North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Lupinus rivularis var. viridifolius include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and provide relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a topical application for skin conditions and wounds.
